result:
I would give every child the right to be believed.  
Not coddled, not shielded from failure—just met with the reflex that their words, feelings, and questions deserve a first, unguarded hearing. A world that begins with “I believe you” wires brains for trust, lowers the volume on shame, and turns schools, courts, kitchens, and parliaments into places where problems are aired before they harden into generational certainties. Every injustice I can name—poverty, abuse, ecological apathy, scientific denial, even war—has a moment in its back-story when someone small tried to describe the water they were swimming in and was waved off. Remove that single reflex to dismiss, and the rest of the fixes start becoming imaginable.

prompt: If you could change the world in one way, what would it be?
